title: Tip 1 for AA
instruction: Open your mouth wide like you are at the dentist.
commonErrors: Reducing it to a schwa in stressed positions.

title: Tip 2 for AA
instruction: Keep the tongue flat.
commonErrors: Reducing it to a schwa in stressed positions.

title: Tip 1 for AH
instruction: Relax your jaw completely.
commonErrors: Pronouncing it as a full open /a/ at the end of words.

title: Tip 2 for AH
instruction: This is the sound of the 'a' at the end of almost every Portuguese word ending in 'a'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing it as a full open /a/ at the end of words.

title: Tip 1 for EY
instruction: Smile slightly and hold the sound steady.
commonErrors: Adding a 'y' glide at the end (making it a diphthong like in 'day').

title: Tip 2 for EY
instruction: Do not move your jaw while saying it.
commonErrors: Adding a 'y' glide at the end (making it a diphthong like in 'day').

title: Tip 3 for EY
instruction: Think of the French 'é'.
commonErrors: Adding a 'y' glide at the end (making it a diphthong like in 'day').

title: Tip 1 for EH
instruction: Drop your jaw more than you think you need to.

title: Tip 2 for EH
instruction: Keep the sound short and flat.

title: Tip 1 for IY
instruction: Stretch your lips into a wide smile.
commonErrors: Making it too short like 'sit' (should be tenser).

title: Tip 2 for IY
instruction: It's the 'ee' sound in 'cheese'.
commonErrors: Making it too short like 'sit' (should be tenser).

title: Tip 1 for OW
instruction: Round your lips into a small 'o' shape.
commonErrors: Adding a 'w' glide at the end.

title: Tip 2 for OW
instruction: Freeze your jaw; do not let it close as you finish the sound.
commonErrors: Adding a 'w' glide at the end.

title: Tip 1 for AO
instruction: Drop your jaw while keeping lips rounded.

title: Tip 2 for AO
instruction: Ideally, put a finger between your teeth to ensure openness.

title: Tip 1 for UW
instruction: Make a 'kissing' face.
commonErrors: Relaxing lips too much (making it sound like 'book').

title: Tip 2 for UW
instruction: Push your lips forward.
commonErrors: Relaxing lips too much (making it sound like 'book').

title: Tip 1 for AN_NASAL
instruction: Touch your nose; you should feel vibration.
commonErrors: Pronouncing the 'n' or 'm' consonant fully (e.g., saying 'ban-da' instead of 'bã-da').

title: Tip 2 for AN_NASAL
instruction: Do not let your tongue touch the roof of your mouth for the 'n'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing the 'n' or 'm' consonant fully (e.g., saying 'ban-da' instead of 'bã-da').

title: Tip 1 for EN_NASAL
instruction: Start to say 'aim' but pinch your nose shut halfway through.

title: Tip 2 for EN_NASAL
instruction: Keep the tongue flat.

title: Tip 1 for IN_NASAL
instruction: Smile wide and buzz the sound in your nose.
commonErrors: Pronouncing the 'm' or 'n' at the end.

title: Tip 2 for IN_NASAL
instruction: It's like a mosquito buzzing 'iii'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing the 'm' or 'n' at the end.

title: Tip 1 for ON_NASAL
instruction: Round lips tightly.

title: Tip 2 for ON_NASAL
instruction: Imagine the sound resonating in your forehead.

title: Tip 1 for UN_NASAL
instruction: Make a fish face and grunt through your nose.

title: Tip 1 for P
instruction: Hold a piece of paper in front of your mouth; it should NOT move when you say 'pão'.
commonErrors: Too much aspiration (puff of air) like in 'pin'.

title: Tip 1 for B
instruction: Fully voice the sound from the throat.

title: Tip 1 for T
instruction: Touch your tongue to your teeth, not the roof of your mouth.
commonErrors: Touching the gum ridge (alveolar) like in English.

title: Tip 2 for T
instruction: No puff of air.
commonErrors: Touching the gum ridge (alveolar) like in English.

title: Tip 1 for D
instruction: Push tongue against teeth.
commonErrors: Touching the gum ridge instead of teeth.

title: Tip 1 for K
instruction: Keep it dry and short.

title: Tip 2 for K
instruction: No explosion of air.

title: Tip 1 for G
instruction: Vibrate vocal cords.

title: Tip 1 for CH
instruction: In Brazil, when 't' is followed by an 'i' sound (like in 'leite'), it becomes 'chi'.
commonErrors: Using it when not appropriate (only used for 't' before 'i' or 'e' in many accents, or spelled 'ch' which is actually /ʃ/). Note: Spelled 'ch' is SH sound. This phoneme is usually T before I.

title: Tip 2 for CH
instruction: Think 'chee'.
commonErrors: Using it when not appropriate (only used for 't' before 'i' or 'e' in many accents, or spelled 'ch' which is actually /ʃ/). Note: Spelled 'ch' is SH sound. This phoneme is usually T before I.

title: Tip 1 for JH
instruction: Whenever 'd' is followed by 'i' (or final 'e'), say 'j' like 'jeep'.
commonErrors: Using /ʒ/ (like measure) instead of the affricate.

title: Tip 2 for JH
instruction: Cidade becomes 'Ci-da-jee'.
commonErrors: Using /ʒ/ (like measure) instead of the affricate.

title: Tip 1 for F
instruction: Same as English.

title: Tip 1 for V
instruction: Keep the teeth on the lip and buzz.
commonErrors: Confusing with /b/ (common in Spanish but not Portuguese).

title: Tip 2 for V
instruction: Do not close your lips fully like a 'b'.
commonErrors: Confusing with /b/ (common in Spanish but not Portuguese).

title: Tip 1 for S
instruction: Standard 's' sound.
commonErrors: Pronouncing 's' between vowels as /s/ (it usually becomes /z/).

title: Tip 2 for S
instruction: Remember: 'S' between two vowels sounds like 'Z' (Casa = Caza).
commonErrors: Pronouncing 's' between vowels as /s/ (it usually becomes /z/).

title: Tip 1 for Z
instruction: Buzz like a bee.
commonErrors: Devoicing at the end of words (though rare in BP).

title: Tip 2 for Z
instruction: Use this sound when a single 's' is between vowels.
commonErrors: Devoicing at the end of words (though rare in BP).

title: Tip 1 for SH
instruction: Round your lips slightly and hush.
commonErrors: None phonetically, but orthographically 'x' can be tricky.

title: Tip 2 for SH
instruction: In Portuguese, 'x' often makes this sound.
commonErrors: None phonetically, but orthographically 'x' can be tricky.

title: Tip 1 for ZH
instruction: It is a soft sound, like a French 'J'.
commonErrors: Using /dʒ/ (hard J) instead. English speakers want to say 'Jungle' but it should be soft 'Zhungle'.

title: Tip 2 for ZH
instruction: Never touch your tongue to the roof of your mouth to stop the air.
commonErrors: Using /dʒ/ (hard J) instead. English speakers want to say 'Jungle' but it should be soft 'Zhungle'.

title: Tip 1 for HH
instruction: If a word starts with 'R', pretend it is an 'H'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing 'R' at the start of words like an English 'R'.
Rolling the 'R' (Spanish style) - usually too strong for BP.

title: Tip 2 for HH
instruction: Rio sounds like 'Hio'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing 'R' at the start of words like an English 'R'.
Rolling the 'R' (Spanish style) - usually too strong for BP.

title: Tip 3 for HH
instruction: Double 'RR' is also an 'H'.
commonErrors: Pronouncing 'R' at the start of words like an English 'R'.
Rolling the 'R' (Spanish style) - usually too strong for BP.

title: Tip 1 for M
instruction: Lips together.
commonErrors: At end of words, it indicates a nasal vowel, not a full 'm' sound.

title: Tip 2 for M
instruction: If at the end of a word (sim), don't close your lips fully, just make the vowel nasal.
commonErrors: At end of words, it indicates a nasal vowel, not a full 'm' sound.

title: Tip 1 for N
instruction: Standard 'n' sound.

title: Tip 1 for NH
instruction: Press the FLAT middle part of your tongue against the roof of your mouth.

title: Tip 2 for NH
instruction: It is one single sound, not two.

title: Tip 3 for NH
instruction: Think of the Spanish 'ñ'.

title: Tip 1 for L
instruction: At the start of a word, use a light, forward 'L'.
commonErrors: Using the dark 'L' (from 'ball') at the start of words.
Note: At the END of words, L becomes 'W' (Brazil) -> 'Braziw'.

title: Tip 2 for L
instruction: At the end of a word, turn it into a 'U' or 'W' sound.
commonErrors: Using the dark 'L' (from 'ball') at the start of words.
Note: At the END of words, L becomes 'W' (Brazil) -> 'Braziw'.

title: Tip 1 for LH
instruction: Touch the middle of your tongue to the roof of your mouth.

title: Tip 2 for LH
instruction: It's the liquid version of 'NH'.

title: Tip 3 for LH
instruction: Say 'million' but freeze on the 'lli' part.

title: Tip 1 for R_TAP
instruction: Don't roll it. Don't growl it.
commonErrors: Using the 'H' sound (which is for double RR).

title: Tip 2 for R_TAP
instruction: Just a quick tap, exactly like American 'water' or 'better'.
commonErrors: Using the 'H' sound (which is for double RR).

title: Tip 1 for W
instruction: Often appears at the end of words ending in 'L' (Brazil = Braziw) or 'O' diphthongs (Pão = Paw-ng).

title: Tip 1 for Y
instruction: Standard 'y' sound.

title: Tip 2 for Y
instruction: Used in diphthongs like 'pai' (father).
